name: Epicurious Carrot Ginger Dressing
directions: Pulse carrots in a food processor until finely ground (almost puréed). Add chopped ginger, chopped shallots, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, and salt and pulse until ginger and shallots are minced. With motor running, add vegetable oil in a slow stream.
Transfer mixture to a blender. Add 1/4 cup water and blend until smooth, 2 to 3 minutes. Thin dressing with additional water if desired.
Dressing keeps, covered and chilled, 1 week.

name: Carrot Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender and pulse until the dressing coats a back of a spoon.
Enjoy over any salad with lots of cabbage!
Bon Apetite.

name: Carrot Ginger Soy Dressing
directions: Add ingredients into blender.
Blend until smooth.  Chill and serve over greens.

name: Japanese Take-Out Ginger Salad Dressing
directions: Combine the vinegar, tomato paste, soy sauce, salt, ginger, sugar, sesame oil, celery, and onion in a blender.  Pulse several times, until the celery and onion are finely chopped.  When the blender running, slowing drizzle in the canola oil.

name: Carrot Ginger Miso Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender, combine all ingredients except oil and process on high.
Add oil in a steady stream until well mixed.
Refrigerate.

name: Carrot-Ginger Dressing
directions: Puree all the ingredients in a food processor until smooth. Use immediately, or refrigerate, covered, for up to 1 day.

name: Japanese Ginger Salad
directions: Combine the oil, soy sauce, vinegar, sesame seeds, ginger, onion, celery, sugar and ketchup in a blender or food processor and process until smooth.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad greens in a salad bowl and toss to coat. Serve immediately.

name: Pete's Ginger Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender on high speed. Blend till well pureed.
When I cook, I cook by sight -- therefore the ingredient amounts are.
estimated. When preparing, taste dressing and adjust ingredients.
accordingly.

name: Carrot Miso Dressing
directions: In a small saucepan, boil the carrot juice until it is reduced to 3 tablespoons, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a blender and let cool slightly.
Add the chopped carrot, ginger, lime juice, soy sauce and miso and blend until the carrot is minced. With the machine on, add the oil in a thin, steady stream.
Season with cayenne and transfer to a glass jar.
Applications: Use as a dipping sauce for vegetables or boiled shrimp. Serve with poached or grilled salmon or tuna.
Toss with cold soba noodles, steamed broccoli or snow peas. Drizzle over grilled chicken breasts or pork tenderloin.
Make Ahead: The dressing can be refrigerated for up to 5 days.
